heading: Treatment of funded welfare benefit plans
label: 419 Treatment of funded welfare benefit plans

L. 99–514, title XVIII, § 1851(a)(12)100 Stat. 2862July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [enacting this subpart] shall apply to contributions paid or accrued after , in taxable years ending after such date.December 31, 1985“(2).—Special rule for collective bargaining agreementsIn the case of plan maintained pursuant to 1 or more collective bargaining agreements—“(A)between employee representatives and 1 or more employers, and“(B)in effect on (or ratified on or before such date),July 1, 1985the amendments made by this section shall not apply to years beginning before the date on which the last of the collective bargaining agreements relating to the plan terminates (determined without regard to any extension thereof agreed to after ).July 1, 1985“(3).—Special rule for paragraph (2)For purposes of paragraph (2), any plan amendment made pursuant to a collective bargaining agreement relating to the plan which amends the plan solely to conform to any requirement added by this section shall not be treated as a termination of such collective bargaining agreement.“(4).—Special effective date for contributions of facilitiesNotwithstanding paragraphs (1) and (2), the amendments made by this section shall apply in the case of—“(A)any contribution after , of a facility to a welfare benefit fund, andJune 22, 1984“(B)any other contribution after , to a welfare benefit fund to be used to acquire or improve a facility.June 22, 1984“(5).—Binding contract exceptions to paragraph (4)Paragraph (4) shall not apply to any facility placed in service before —January 1, 1987“(A)which is acquired or improved by the fund (or contributed to the fund) pursuant to a binding contract in effect on , and at all times thereafter, orJune 22, 1984“(B)the construction of which by or for the fund began before .June 22, 1984“(6).—Amendments related to tax on unrelated business incomeThe amendments made by subsection (b) [amending ] shall apply with respect to taxable years ending after . For purposes of section 15 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986], such amendments shall be treated as a change in the rate of a tax imposed by chapter 1 of such Code.section 512 of this titleDecember 31, 1985“(7).—Amendments related to excise taxes on certain welfare benefit plansThe amendments made by subsection (c) [enacting ] shall apply to benefits provided after .”section 4976 of this titleDecember 31, 1985

Plan Amendments Not Required Until January 1, 1989For provisions directing that if any amendments made by subtitle A or subtitle C of title XI [§§ 1101–1147 and 1171–1177] or title XVIII [§§ 1800–1899A] of require an amendment to any plan, such plan amendment shall not be required to be made before the first plan year beginning on or after , see , as amended, set out as a note under .Pub. L. 99–514section 1140 of Pub. L. 99–514section 401 of this titleJan. 1, 1989

Effective Date of Regulations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XVIII, § 1851(a)(8)(B)100 Stat. 2860Oct. 22, 1986“Except in the case of a reserve for post-retirement medical or life insurance benefits and any other arrangement between an insurance company and an employer under which the employer has a contractual right to a refund or dividend based solely on the experience of such employer, any account held for an employer by any person and defined as a fund in regulations issued pursuant to section 419(e)(3)(C) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986] shall be considered a ‘fund’ no earlier than 6 months following the date such regulations are published in final form.”

L. 99–514, title XVIII, § 1851(a)(8)(B)100 Stat. 2860Oct. 22, 1986“Except in the case of a reserve for post-retirement medical or life insurance benefits and any other arrangement between an insurance company and an employer under which the employer has a contractual right to a refund or dividend based solely on the experience of such employer, any account held for an employer by any person and defined as a fund in regulations issued pursuant to section 419(e)(3)(C) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986] shall be considered a ‘fund’ no earlier than 6 months following the date such regulations are published in final form.”

children.text: shall not be deductible under this chapter, but
if they would otherwise be deductible, shall (subject to the limitation of subsection (b)) be deductible under this section for the taxable year in which paid.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: b Limitation
children.text: The amount of the deduction allowable under subsection (a)(2) for any taxable year shall not exceed the welfare benefit fund’s qualified cost for the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: the qualified direct cost for such taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: subject to the limitation of section 419A(b), any addition to a qualified asset account for the taxable year.

children.heading: Reduction for funds after-tax income
children.label: 2 Reduction for funds after-tax income
children.text: In the case of any welfare benefit fund, the qualified cost for any taxable year shall be reduced by such fund’s after-tax income for such taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: such benefits were provided directly by the employer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the employer used the cash receipts and disbursements method of accounting.

children.heading: Time when benefits provided
children.label: B Time when benefits provided
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), a benefit shall be treated as provided when such benefit would be includible in the gross income of the employee if provided directly by the employer (or would be so includible but for any provision of this chapter excluding such benefit from gross income).

children.heading: 60-month amortization of child care facilities
children.label: C 60-month amortization of child care facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In determining qualified direct costs with respect to any child care facility for purposes of subparagraph (A), in lieu of depreciation the adjusted basis of such facility shall be allowable as a deduction ratably over a period of 60 months beginning with the month in which the facility is placed in service.

children.label: I
children.text: not of a character subject to depreciation; or

children.label: II
children.text: located outside the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: the deductions allowed by this chapter which are directly connected with the production of such gross income, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the tax imposed by this chapter on the fund for the taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: contributions and other amounts received from employees shall be taken into account, but

children.label: ii
children.text: contributions from the employer shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: Item only taken into account once
children.label: 5 Item only taken into account once
children.text: No item may be taken into account more than once in determining the qualified cost of any welfare benefit fund.
the amount of the contributions paid (or deemed paid under this subsection) by the employer during any taxable year to a welfare benefit fund, exceeds
the limitation of subsection (b),

children.label: A
children.text: which is part of a plan of an employer, and

children.label: B
children.text: through which the employer provides welfare benefits to employees or their beneficiaries.

children.label: A
children.text: section 83(h) applies,

children.label: B
children.text: section 404 applies (determined without regard to section 404(b)(2)), or

children.label: C
children.text: section 404A applies.

children.label: A
children.text: any organization described in paragraph (7), (9), or (17) of section 501(c),

children.label: B
children.text: any trust, corporation, or other organization not exempt from the tax imposed by this chapter, and

children.label: C
children.text: to the extent provided in regulations, any account held for an employer by any person.

children.heading: Treatment of amounts held pursuant to certain insurance contracts
children.label: 4 Treatment of amounts held pursuant to certain insurance contracts
i
children.text: such contract is a life insurance contract described in section 264(a)(1), or

children.label: ii
children.text: such contract is a qualified nonguaranteed contract.

children.label: I
children.text: there is no guarantee of a renewal of such contract, and

children.label: II
children.text: other than insurance protection, the only payments to which the employer or employees are entitled are experience rated refunds or policy dividends which are not guaranteed and which are determined by factors other than the amount of welfare benefits paid to (or on behalf of) the employees of the employer or their beneficiaries.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: In the case of any qualified nonguaranteed contract, subparagraph (A) shall not apply unless the amount of any experience rated refund or policy dividend payable to an employer with respect to a policy year is treated by the employer as received or accrued in the taxable year in which the policy year ends.

children.heading: Method of contributions, etc., having the effect of a plan
children.label: f Method of contributions, etc., having the effect of a plan

children.text: there is no plan, but
there is a method or arrangement of employer contributions or benefits which has the effect of a plan,

children.heading: Extension to plans for independent contractors
children.label: g Extension to plans for independent contractors

children.text: this section shall apply as if there were such a relationship, and

any reference in this section to the employer shall be treated as a reference to the person for whom services are provided, and any reference in this section to an employee shall be treated as a reference to the person providing the services.
